rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IN THE MATTER OF MASHA LEVINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner'S Request To Release Property From Claim Of Lien And Related Relief
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 15, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER'S REQUEST TO RELEASE PROPERTY FROM CLAIM OF LIEN AND RELATED RELIEF is GRANTED. Reasonable and necessary attorney's fees granted in the amount of $1,200 (4 hours at $300/hour; the Court notes that hourly rate and billing information was not provided, just a lump sum) and costs in the amount of $495 (first appearance fee and motion fee). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HAWTHORNE PLACE OWNERS ASSOCIATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Hearing On Verified Petition To Reduce Required Voting Percentage To Amend Declaration Of Conditions, Covenants And Restrictions Of Hawthorne Place, A Condominium Project
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 15, 2022 line 2. PETITIONER'S AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR HEARING ON VERIFIED PETITION TO REDUCE REQUIRED VOTING PERCENTAGE TO AMEND DECLARATION OF CONDITIONS, COVENANTS AND RESTRICTIONS OF HAWTHORNE PLACE, A CONDOMINIUM PROJECT is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. The supplemental declaration which appears to be filed as a substitute for a proof of service is not signed. Furthermore, while the title of the Motion appears to seek substantive relief as requested in the Petition, the body of the Notice only requests "an order setting a hearing" without any suggested hearing date or briefing schedule. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: YOTAM SHMUELOV VS. ANNA PAPOULIAS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 15, 2022 line 4. DEFENDANTS ANNA PAPOULIAS, VICTOR MAKRAS AND VICTOR G. MAKRAS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and CONTINUED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to Victor Makras and Victor G. Markas, Inc. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that "landlord" as used in CC 1942.4 is defined by SFRO and not by CC 1940.8.5. Furthermore, Plaintiff fails to provide no evidence and no legal theory upon which Makras may be held liable as alter ego of Anna Papoulias, an individual. Motion is continued on the issue of CC 1942.4 (2) to October 13, 2022, the last day to hear a motion for summary adjudication under CCP 437c. No later than September 27, 2022 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed two pages) and admissible competent evidence regarding CC 1942.4(2). No later than October 4, 2022 Defendant to file a supplemental reply brief (not to exceed 2 pages) and objections to evidence, if any. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MIRIAN ORELLANA VS. EVELYN R. MATA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Case From Unlimited To Limited Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 15, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT EVELYN MATA'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO RECLASSIFY CASE FROM UNLIMITED TO LIMITED JURISDICTION is DENIED. Moving party failed to establish that meeting the jurisdictional limit is not possible in this case, i.e. that this case will be resolved before the demanded rent and daily damages reach $25,000. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
